title: fragaria vesca subsp. bracteata
intro: Fragaria vesca subsp. bracteata, also known as the Alpine strawberry or Strawberry-leaf, is a subspecies of the wild strawberry plant native to Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which contain flavonoids and salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The leaves of Fragaria vesca subsp. bracteata have been used to treat fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, offering various health benefits such as reducing pain and promoting wound healing. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, although it can also be consumed as a food source, particularly its edible leaves and fruits.
